sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Follow-up on chronic medical conditions and fatigue.
History of Present Illness
Mr. Walker presents for a routine follow-up regarding his chronic medical conditions. He underwent an aortic valve replacement and ascending aortic aneurysm repair on 1/22/2013. He reports experiencing increased fatigue, particularly at the end of the day, but continues to ride his bike, though at a slower pace. He states he can ride approximately 20-30 miles, but often needs to take breaks. He mentions a nodule on his right testicle and expresses concern about it. He denies any recent trauma. He reports taking vitamin D3 and clindamycin prior to dental procedures. He has been vaccinated against COVID-19. He notes that his wife and friends have observed pursed lip breathing when he breathes, and he's unsure if this is a habit.
Findings
Physical exam reveals pursed lip breathing and low exhalation phase. The right testicle is palpably with a two to three millimeter nodule. The remainder of the physical exam is unremarkable. The patient's alkaline phosphatase level is elevated at 156. The patient's total cholesterol is 247, HDL is 66, LDL is 166, and triglycerides are 74. His TSH is normal at 2.68. His CBC is unremarkable. His most recent vitamin D level is at the high end of normal at 94. The patient's lung field is abnormal.
Assessment

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
The patient's primary concerns include chronic fatigue, a testicular nodule, and an elevated alkaline phosphatase level. He has a history of aortic valve replacement and ascending aortic aneurysm repair.
Plan
Order an echocardiogram and a stress test. Order a pulmonary function test. Refer to urology for evaluation of the testicular nodule. Recommend a wellness visit in approximately one year. Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks if symptoms persist. Continue acetaminophen as needed for pain. Ensure the patient receives an MMR vaccine and a shingles vaccine.
